name: Skirrid Fawr (Ysgyryd Fawr)
terrain: Stony path through woods then steep grass
highlights: 'Holy Mountain' with landslip ridge and ruined medieval St Michael's chapel on top.
points_of_interest: Ruined chapel of St Michael (pilgrimage site)
viewpoints: Panoramic views over Abergavenny and the Usk Valley
parking_start: NT Skirrid car park on B4521
food_drink_nearby: The Walnut Tree; Skirrid Mountain Inn (oldest pub in Wales)
